title: Pentagon Reiterates Coordination Posture; Luna Threatens Subpoena as 33 Days Remain to EO Deadline
detail: The Pentagon reiterated its standard coordination statement — 'AARO is working in close coordination with the White House and across federal agencies' — without providing any UAP videos or a release timeline. Rep. Anna Paulina Luna indicated she may invoke House Oversight Committee subpoena power if the Department of War continues to stall on the 46 classified UAP videos. The Pentagon acknowledged it had not responded to Luna's original March 31 letter until she reached out directly, lending credibility to her earlier 'how convenient' characterization. With only 33 days remaining until Trump's executive order 90-day milestone (May 20, 2026), the gap between White House rhetoric and Pentagon action remained the central tension in the disclosure process.
confidenceReason: Pentagon statement confirmed by Newsweek; subpoena threat reported by multiple outlets

media.caption: Pentagon under growing pressure over UAP video release as EO deadline approaches
